title: The Honest and the Thief
description: Two theories have been published about ingredients you've fully studied. Check each claim against the aspect clues — not every publication is wrong. Expose only the one that is.

hints.text: Each published ingredient has all three aspect clues. Determine the true alchemical for each from its clues, then compare it to what the publication claims. Only a claim where a sign differs from the clues needs to be debunked.

ing1 has R-, G+, B- → alchemical npN. The publication claims npN — all three signs match. This publication is accurate; no debunk needed. ing4 has R-, G+, B+ → alchemical nPp. The publication claims pnP (R+, G-, B+). ing4's R- contradicts the claimed R+, and G+ contradicts the claimed G-. This publication is false.

One-step plan: apprentice debunk on ing4, reveal R (or G). The true sign is R- (from the clue). The publication claims pnP which has R+. Since R- ≠ R+, the publication is removed. The publication for ing1 correctly describes npN and is left untouched.
